steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Similar figures have equal corresponding angles
steps.content: When two figures are similar, all their corresponding angles are equal. The scale factor — the ratio comparing side lengths — applies only to linear dimensions (the sides), never to angles. This is a fundamental property of similarity.

steps.title: Use the angle correspondence to find angle W
steps.content: We are told that angle P corresponds to angle W. Since PQRS is similar to WXYZ, the corresponding angles must be equal. We're given that angle P measures 48°, therefore angle W also measures 48°. That confirms B is correct.
